mnemonics: C.A.R.E.S. - Create An engaging Respectful Environment and Set clear expectations
P.O.W.E.R. - Prepare Others to Understand Results Effectively
keyPoints: Introduction: Introduce the topic and set the context (5-7 minutes)
Explanation: Explain the key concepts and theory behind strictly competitive games (20-25 minutes)
Activity: Engage students with a game related to the topic (20-25 minutes)
Evaluation: Assess students' understanding through a quiz or group discussion (10-15 minutes)
Conclusion: Summarize the key points and provide opportunities for students to ask questions
Role of PowerPoint: Use engaging images, diagrams and charts to support the explanation and make it more engaging
Tips for the lecturer: Encourage active learning, provide examples and use real-life scenarios to make the topic more relevant
